1.11a: Instructions are issued via email by Legal Marketing Services Limited (LMS) and must be accessed through the LMS Conveyancer Zone.
Solicitors do not receive a separate mortgage offer addressed to the firm; the borrower's copy of the offer serves as the instruction.
Mortgage offers include a 7-day reflection period which the borrower may waive.
Standard legal documents and the 'Mortgage Lending Instructions & Guidance Notes' must be obtained from the LMS website.
Offers dated from 16 June 2024 require the HSBC Mortgage Loan Terms and Conditions 2023 v1 and the R series mortgage deed.
1.7: Security is only accepted where the Borrowers and Mortgagors are identical parties using an A1 mortgage deed.
1.14: A firm may act if a member or their family is the seller, provided a separate unrelated partner acts for the bank and no conflict of interest exists.
1.15: A firm may act if a member or their family is the borrower, provided a separate unrelated partner acts for the bank and no conflict of interest exists.
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative's name and address is not required.
3.1.4: The bank must be notified only if the seller is unrepresented or if identity checks on the seller's representative are unsatisfactory.
3.1.5: No alternative identity verification documents are specified as acceptable.
4.1: Valuation reports are for bank use only and must not be shared with the borrower.
If the valuer is SDLAVM or SDLMDV, the solicitor must disregard sections 2-7 and 10, using only sections 1 and 9.
4.3: The bank aims to respond to property or valuation queries within 3 working days.
Funds must not be requested until all valuation queries are resolved.
5.2.1: Assignable contracts and irrevocable powers of attorney for intervening sellers are prohibited.
Sub-sales, back-to-back transactions, and contract assignments are not acceptable.
5.4.4: Japanese Knotweed within 3 meters of the boundary must be reported if not noted in the valuation.
If present on-site, damage to outbuildings or fences must be minor.
Requirements for onsite knotweed: Treatment plan from an accredited body (e.g., PCA, INNSA), a Completion Certificate, and a 10-year insurance-backed guarantee.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if the firm is CoPSO registered, maintains adequate PI insurance, and complies with the Search Code.
5.4.6: Local authority search indemnity insurance is accepted for property valuations up to £1 million.
Search indemnity policy must cover the open market value.
No other types of search indemnity insurance are accepted.
5.5.3a: Private drainage systems must be compliant with current legislation.
Shared drainage systems require legal arrangements for access and maintenance.
5.5.3b: The bank does not require copies of planning permissions.
Indemnity insurance for planning breaches is at the conveyancer's discretion per Section 9.
5.5.3c: The bank does not require copies of building regulation consents.
5.5.3d: Certificate of lawful use is required for commercial-to-residential conversions if planning permission is unconfirmed.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are accepted if mutual rights of support, repair, maintenance, and entry exist along with enforceable covenants.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are generally not acceptable unless they involve a share of freehold with a leasehold interest or are qualified Coach Houses.
5.8.5: For Tyneside-style flat arrangements, the bank requires a charge over both the leasehold and the freehold reversion titles owned by the borrower.
5.13.1: Deposits from unsecured credit or personal borrowing are prohibited.
Family gifted deposits from close relatives (spouse, parent, sibling, etc.) do not need reporting if unconditional and non-repayable.
Gifts from friends or employers are not accepted.
Gift letters must be dated within 3 months of completion.
Draft Declarations of Trust must be submitted 10 working days prior to completion for approval.
Bankruptcy searches are mandatory for all parties contributing to the purchase price.
5.14.1: Leasehold properties must have at least 50 years remaining at the end of the mortgage term.
Leases with less than 85 years remaining at the outset require valuer approval.
5.14.9: Ground rent must not exceed 0.2% of the property value.
Leases granted after 30 June 2022 must be limited to a peppercorn rent.
Ground rent review periods must be 10 years or greater.
Escalation of ground rent must be linked to RPI or less.
Ground rent doubling is only acceptable if it occurs every 20 years or more and ceases after 125 years.
Indemnity insurance is not an alternative for non-compliant ground rent; a deed of variation is required.
5.14.12: Receipts for ground rent or service charges are not required.
Retentions for estimated service charges must be limited to 30 days after final accounts are issued.
5.14.17b: The bank must be notified before completion if the owner is a non-qualifying leaseholder under the Building Safety Act 2022.
EWS1 reports older than 5 years are acceptable if no newer FRAEW exists.
No referral is needed for masonry buildings or EWS1 ratings of A1, A2, or B1.
5.15.2a: Estate rent charges are acceptable if they exclude Law of Property Act s121, include a 28-day mortgagee notice period, or are managed by a resident-owned company.
Indemnity insurance is not accepted for estate rent charges.
5.17.5a: Original or certified copies of Power of Attorney and a specimen signature must be provided before completion.
5.19.1: Shared ownership schemes (NBHB, FTBI) are accepted.
Shared equity, Open Market Homebuy, and Discount for Sale schemes are prohibited.
Shared ownership leases must include a mortgagee protection clause covering 18 months' interest and 3% market value for fees.
5.20.1: Solar PV panel leases must meet UK Finance minimum standards or the case cannot proceed.
6.4.4: Financial incentives up to 5% of the purchase price do not need reporting.
Reporting is required for cash incentives >5%, non-fixed incentives (e.g., vehicles), or part-exchange transactions.
A UK Finance Disclosure of Incentives Form is mandatory.
6.6.1: Buy-to-Let tenancies must be ASTs (max 3 years), Wales Standard Contracts (max 3 years), or Company Lets (max 12 months).
Lending on Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO) is prohibited.
6.7.4: Professional Consultant's Certificates (PCC) are only accepted for developments of 10 properties or fewer.
6.8.1: Unadopted roads are acceptable if adoption plans exist or a management company is responsible; no bond or retention is required.
6.12.1: Lending is prohibited on properties subject to overage agreements.
7.3: Consent is required for occupants aged 17+ and those with a financial interest, excluding students away from home.
A maximum of 2 lodgers in one room is permitted.
The Homes for Ukraine scheme does not require a Letter of Consent or Postponement.
8.1: Independent Legal Advice is mandatory for non-borrowing legal owners if the loan portion they do not benefit from exceeds £50,000.
9.2: Indemnity insurance must cover the full open market value of the property.
10.2b: A minimum of 6 working days' notice is required for the mortgage advance.
The Certificate of Title must be submitted via the LMS Conveyancer Zone Portal.
10.3: Capitalised booking fees must be deducted from the gross loan amount to determine the available funds for completion.
10.7: Delayed completion funds must be returned via CHAPS within 3 working days.
14.2.2: Post-completion documents required: Register completion sheet, A2 Indirect Mortgage Deed (if used), Indemnity Policies, and Letters of Consent.
16.3.1: Transfers of equity require a new mortgage deed; the bank will not sign the transfer deed.
17.1.1: Redemption statements must be requested through the LMS Conveyancer Zone smart form.
17.2.1b: Discharges are processed electronically by the bank directly with the Land Registry.
